figures.caption: Table 3: Reference performance of annotation-required methods and our annotation-free method on nuScenes and SemanticKITTI.
Table 1: Quantitative comparison with annotation-free SOTA methods on the nuScenes dataset. The best result in each column is shown in bold. ↑ denotes higher is better.

Figure 4: (a) and (b) show the visualized comparisons on the nuScenes and SemanticKITTI respectively, while (c) and (d) present the box-plot distributions of per-class IoU on the two datasets. The box represents the interquartile range, the blue solid line inside represents the median, and⋆ represents the mean value.
Figure 5: (a), (b), and (c) respectively depict BEV segmentation maps of nuScenes traffic scenes under sunny, nighttime, and rainy conditions.
